description: Mechanical verification registry for documented cpersona issues. Source of truth for bug-existence proof; checked by scripts/verify-issues.sh (mirrors the ClotoCore infrastructure).

issues.summary: (Bug A) Embedding env vars are read with the CPERSONA_ prefix only, but the marketplace catalog declares the generic EMBEDDING_MODE / EMBEDDING_HTTP_URL keys; an installed cpersona therefore runs with embeddings silently off and recall degrades to FTS-only
issues.fix_note: config.py now reads the generic EMBEDDING_MODE / EMBEDDING_HTTP_URL keys with a CPERSONA_* override (specific > generic, matching the CScheduler convention and the marketplace catalog), so a catalog-installed cpersona enables embeddings instead of silently running FTS-only. Pending version bump.

issues.summary: (Bug B) FTS keyword search builds the MATCH expression with query.split(); space-less scripts (Japanese/Chinese) collapse into a single phrase the trigram index never matches, so the keyword retriever is dead for Japanese and RRF loses its discriminating channel (recall-contamination precondition). Fix must pair a trigram query builder with a bm25-magnitude-aware merge (see ClotoCore/docs/RECALL_CONTAMINATION_AB_2026-06-14.md sec 10).
issues.fix_note: Replaced query.split() with _build_fts_query() (CJK trigram-OR decomposition) so the trigram FTS index is exercised for space-less scripts. Paired with the RSF merge (RECALL_MODE=rsf) which keeps the keyword channel's bm25 magnitude. Pending version bump.

issues.summary: (CRITICAL) HTTP Bearer auth bypass: BearerTokenMiddleware only rejects a request when the Authorization header is present-but-wrong; the `elif auth_token and not header: pass` branch lets header-less requests through to the app. With auth_token set and the default 0.0.0.0:8402 bind, any client on the network can call destructive tools (delete_agent_data, import_memories) with no token. Predates the package restructure (present in bf59f03^) and HTTP transport is entirely untested.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: BearerTokenMiddleware now rejects any missing/blank/malformed token with 401 (the header-less bypass branch is gone) and compares tokens with hmac.compare_digest. Regression: test_v2435_bugfixes.py auth tests + test_server_middleware_source_has_no_bypass_branch.

issues.summary: (HIGH) Schema migration swallows every ALTER failure with a bare `except Exception: pass` and then unconditionally stamps the latest SCHEMA_VERSION at the end. A transient failure (database is locked / disk I/O) leaves the column missing but the version marked current, so the migration is never retried and dependent writes (e.g. channel column) break permanently.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: migrations use idempotent _ensure_column existence checks (no swallowed ALTER errors) and the version stamp is withheld unless the ladder completes (guarded by `migration_error is None`), so a transient failure retries next boot. Regression: test_schema_v9_migration.py.

issues.summary: (HIGH) Task-queue poison-pill: _fetch_next runs json.loads(payload_json) outside the _loop try block. A single malformed payload row raises in the background task (started via asyncio.create_task with no done-callback), which dies silently; the row is not removed, so after a process restart the ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1 head re-crashes and the queue is permanently stuck with no log output.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: _fetch_next catches JSONDecodeError, discards the poison row and advances; start() attaches a done-callback surfacing abnormal loop exits; the drain body is wrapped so a transient fault re-arms instead of killing the loop. Pattern is the fix marker (additive fix). Regression: test_v2435_bugfixes.py poison-pill tests.

issues.summary: (HIGH) Queue-path archive_episode is a silent no-op that loses data. server.py enqueues an archive task only when summary is empty, but the dequeue side do_archive_episode returns immediately when summary is empty (returns {ok:True, episode_id:None}) without writing the episode. The task is deleted as completed and the caller gets {ok:true, queued:true}, so the loss is unobservable. Dead path left over after the server-side LLM summariser was removed.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: do_archive_episode_or_queue no longer enqueues an empty-summary archive (which the worker completed as a no-op, dropping the episode); it returns an explicit ok:false error. do_archive_episode's empty-summary path likewise returns ok:false. Regression: test_task_queue.py::test_tool_archive_episode_without_summary_is_rejected_not_queued.

issues.summary: (HIGH) check_health(fix=true) duplicate_content repair runs a global DELETE that ignores agent scope and locked rows. The detection query is agent-scoped, but the DELETE (id NOT IN SELECT MIN(id) GROUP BY agent_id, content) spans all agents and can delete locked memories, violating the lock_memory contract. Monthly maintenance for one agent mutates other agents' data.
issues.pattern: DELETE FROM memories WHERE id NOT IN \(SELECT MIN\(id\) FROM memories GROUP BY agent_id, content\)
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: the duplicate_content repair DELETE is now agent-scoped and filters `locked = 0`, so it never touches another agent's rows or a lock-protected memory. Regression: test_v2435_bugfixes.py check_health dedup tests.

issues.summary: (HIGH) memories_fts desync: the memories FTS5 external-content table has AFTER INSERT/DELETE triggers but no AFTER UPDATE trigger (episodes_fts has all three). do_update_memory compensates with a manual UPDATE of memories_fts run after the base content is already rewritten, which leaves stale trigrams in the index (and swallows errors with except: pass). check_health's desync check only compares COUNTs, so this row-count-preserving contamination is undetectable and old wording keeps matching new content (recall contamination).
issues.pattern: UPDATE memories_fts SET content = \? WHERE rowid = \?
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: memories_fts gains an AFTER UPDATE trigger (SCHEMA_VERSION 10->11, with a one-time index rebuild) so in-place content edits stay in sync; the stale manual FTS UPDATE in do_update_memory is removed. Regression: test_v2435_bugfixes.py::test_update_memory_keeps_fts_in_sync.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) empty_channel fix conflicts with the ''=global channel semantics. maintenance_handlers rewrites channel '' to 'chat', but the recall path and migrate_channel_axis(globalize_unrecoverable=true) treat channel='' as global (matches all channel-scoped recalls). Running check_health(fix=true) after a globalize re-orphans those rows from non-chat channels. Leftover rule from the v6 migration worldview.
issues.fix_note: Fixed in v2.4.36: the empty_channel check/fix was removed from check_health; channel='' is the valid global channel under the ''=global semantics (recall matching + migrate_channel_axis globalize target).

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_store maps the new memory id via 'SELECT id ... ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1' after commit instead of cursor.lastrowid. Under concurrent stores (HTTP mode / queue running alongside) an interleaved INSERT makes it bind the wrong mem_id to the remote vector index, so recall returns a different memory's content (persistent contamination). The dedup check -> INSERT is also non-atomic (TOCTOU) with no UNIQUE constraint, allowing duplicate rows.
issues.pattern: SELECT id FROM memories WHERE agent_id = \? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1
issues.fix_note: Fixed in v2.4.36: do_store uses INSERT OR IGNORE + cursor.lastrowid for the remote index binding; SCHEMA_VERSION 11->12 adds UNIQUE dedup indexes (agent_id, project_id, channel, content) and a partial (agent_id, project_id, msg_id) WHERE msg_id != '' that make the dedup atomic. Migration collapses unlocked exact duplicates first (keep-MIN(id), never-touch-locked) and skips index creation non-fatally if a locked duplicate blocks it.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_update_memory rewrites content but never recomputes the embedding (and does not NULL it), so the stored vector stays for the old text and the row is not caught by check_health's null/dim-mismatch repair. The remote /index is also not updated. An edited rule memory keeps being recalled by its stale embedding.
issues.pattern: UPDATE memories SET content = \? WHERE id = \?
issues.fix_note: Fixed in v2.4.36: do_update_memory re-embeds the new content with the same policy as do_store (NULL on failure or when no client is configured, so check_health null-embedding repair applies) and re-sends the row to the remote /index.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The v11 FTS AFTER UPDATE triggers (memories_fts_au / episodes_au) fire on every column update, so do_recall's per-hit recall_count/last_recalled_at bump rewrites each hit's full trigram posting on every recall: ~20x slower hot-path UPDATE (p50 1.1ms vs 0.05ms, p95 8ms vs 0.07ms on a 2000-row corpus) and unbounded FTS index bloat (~29KB of dead postings per recall, +66% db size over 200 recalls). The maintenance embedding-only UPDATEs on episodes churn the same way.
issues.fix_note: Fixed after v2.4.36 (schema v13): both AU triggers are column-scoped (AFTER UPDATE OF content / OF summary, keywords) and guarded by WHEN old <> new, so recall-count bumps and embedding-only updates no longer touch the FTS index while real content edits still reindex (bug-008 guarantee preserved). Migration drops and recreates the triggers; the index itself is untouched so no rebuild is needed.

issues.summary: (HIGH) _autocut applies Weaviate-style gap detection to rank-fusion scores (_rrf_score/_rsf_score). RRF scores decay hyperbolically by construction, so the boundary between dual-retriever hits (~2/61) and single-retriever hits (~1/61) always shows a ~50% relative gap; on homogeneous corpora with little retriever overlap autocut slices a full recall to 2 rows regardless of the caller's limit (LMEB MemBench multi_session_assistant NDCG@10 = 0.0; +45.2 NDCG recovered with autocut off). The cut discards relevant memories far beyond the intended low-confidence trim.
issues.fix_note: Fixed after v2.4.36: _autocut keys on similarity-scale signals only (confidence when CONFIDENCE_ENABLED sorted the results, cosine for cascade ordering) and returns fusion-ordered results whole — RRF/RSF contamination control is the fused quality gate's job. Cascade and confidence gap-cut behaviour is unchanged (pinned by test_autocut_signal.py).

issues.summary: (HIGH) check_health(fix=true) duplicate_content repair groups only by (agent_id, content) at checks.py:109/121, ignoring the project_id/channel axes of the idx_memories_dedup_content UNIQUE contract; two legitimately distinct memories that share content across different project_id/channel gamma-buckets are collapsed into one duplicate group and every row except the global MIN(id) is DELETEd, silently and permanently destroying valid cross-bucket rows (e.g. the same text under project_id='' and 'cycia-mc') on the recommended monthly maintenance path.
issues.fix_sketch: GROUP BY agent_id, project_id, channel, content in BOTH the detection query and the survivor MIN(id) subquery to match idx_memories_dedup_content and the v12 migration.
issues.note: Initial audit framing (full-key collapse) was imprecise: the broad grouping's cross-CHANNEL cleanup is intentional (test _insert_dup). The real defect was crossing the PROJECT isolation axis; fix adds project_id (not channel) to the grouping.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: check_duplicate_content groups by (agent_id, project_id, content) in both detection and the MIN(id) survivor subquery — deliberately including project_id (a γ relevance axis — agent_id is the only hard isolation boundary, see cpersona/isolation.py, but the same content under different projects is legitimately distinct with different visibility) while still omitting channel (preserving the intended cross-channel cleanup the UNIQUE index leaves to check_health). Cross-project content is no longer collapsed, so the MIN(id) survivor can no longer delete the global copy and narrow another project's visibility. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_duplicate_content_does_not_collapse_across_projects; cross-channel dedup pinned by test_v2435_bugfixes.py.

issues.summary: (HIGH) deep_check(fix=true) deep_short_content selects rows with LENGTH(TRIM(content))<=5 and DELETEs them at checks.py:874 with no locked=0 guard on either the selection (:867) or the DELETE, violating the module docstring's stated bug-007 invariant that fixes never touch locked rows; a short memory the user explicitly locked (e.g. content='ACLs') is irreversibly deleted during routine maintenance.
issues.pattern: DELETE FROM memories WHERE id IN \(\{placeholders\}\) AND locked = 0
issues.fix_sketch: Add `AND locked = 0` to the deep_short_content selection and exclude locked ids from the DELETE.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: deep_short_content's DELETE now excludes locked rows (AND locked = 0) and reports cur.rowcount, so a user-locked short memory survives deep_check(fix=true). Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_locked_rows_survive_destructive_maintenance.

issues.summary: (HIGH) The export/import backup-restore path loses scope and crashes: do_export_memories never selects project_id/channel (admin_handlers.py:1115) and do_import_memories uses a plain INSERT that omits project_id/channel/embedding (:1252), so every restored row collapses into project_id=''/channel=''/NULL-embedding (gamma+channel isolation destroyed, rows invisible to vector recall); and because msg_id-less duplicate content violates the v12 idx_memories_dedup_content UNIQUE(agent_id,project_id,channel,content), the plain INSERT raises an uncaught IntegrityError (only json.JSONDecodeError is caught) that aborts the entire restore, while prior rows sit in the un-rolled-back shared transaction to be flushed by the next handler's commit.
issues.pattern: INSERT INTO memories \(agent_id, msg_id, content, source, timestamp, metadata\)
issues.fix_sketch: Use INSERT OR IGNORE, carry project_id/channel/embedding through export+import, and wrap the import loop in an explicit transaction with rollback on error.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: export now carries project_id/channel/locked (+ embedding via include_embeddings); import restores those columns, decodes the embedding, uses INSERT OR IGNORE (a dedup collision is a counted skip, not an IntegrityError abort), and runs the whole restore in one transaction that rolls back on error. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_export_import_roundtrip_preserves_axes_and_embedding, ::test_import_content_collision_skips_not_aborts.

issues.summary: (HIGH) The streamable-http transport fails open: auth_token defaults to '' (server.py:897) and BearerTokenMiddleware only enforces credentials inside `if auth_token:` (server.py:921), so an unset/empty CPERSONA_AUTH_TOKEN turns the middleware into a no-op while the server binds the default 0.0.0.0:8402 -- any network host can invoke all 24 tools unauthenticated, including delete_agent_data/delete_memory and export_memories/import_memories (caller-controlled file write/read), with no warning and no refusal to start. Distinct from the bug-003 header-less bypass, which only covered the token-set case.
issues.fix_sketch: Fail closed: refuse to start the HTTP transport when auth_token is empty, default CPERSONA_HTTP_HOST to 127.0.0.1, and log loudly whenever auth is disabled.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: HTTP transport fails closed — CPERSONA_HTTP_HOST defaults to 127.0.0.1 and _assert_safe_http_bind refuses an unauthenticated non-loopback bind (SystemExit); a loopback bind without a token warns loudly. The BearerTokenMiddleware `if auth_token:` gate is unchanged (still correct when a token is set); the exposure was the empty-token + public-bind startup, now guarded. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py HTTP-bind tests.

issues.summary: (HIGH) _autocut (memory_handlers.py:452-453) falls to key='_cosine' when the top row lacks _confidence/_rsf/_rrf scores and computes scores=[r.get(key) or 0 ...], so in cascade recall with CONFIDENCE_ENABLED=false (shipped default, the documented topic-drift workaround config) the stage-ordered vector-hit-first result list makes every non-vector (episode/keyword/profile) row score 0; this manufactures a full-scale gap at the vector->non-vector boundary and autocut truncates ALL non-vector hits whenever >=1 vector hit exists, silently collapsing multi-strategy cascade recall to vector-only. Same class as fixed bug-013 but for the cosine branch the rrf/rsf early-return does not cover.
issues.pattern: if any\(r\.get\("_cosine"\) is None for r in results\)
issues.fix_sketch: When the ordering signal is not uniformly present (rows lacking _cosine under cascade/confidence-off), skip autocut or run gap detection only over rows that carry the chosen score, mirroring the rrf/rsf early-return.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: _autocut's cosine fallback branch now returns the list whole when any row lacks _cosine (cascade concatenates non-vector stages that carry no cosine), instead of scoring the gap as 0 and truncating every non-vector hit. Homogeneous cosine-scored and confidence-sorted cuts are unchanged. Regression: test_autocut_signal.py::test_cascade_mixed_signal_is_not_cut.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) auto_tool's _handler (_vendored_mcp_common/mcp_utils.py:127) gates on `if default is not None`, so a param spec with an explicit None default -- ('project_id', str, None), used by recall/recall_with_context/list_memories/list_episodes to mean 'omit -> no filter (all projects)' -- is indistinguishable from having no default and validate_str substitutes ''; an omitted project_id therefore reaches the handler as '' (gamma_clause -> `project_id = ''` = global pool only), making the documented all-projects branch unreachable via MCP and silently excluding every project-bucketed (e.g. cycia-mc) row from session-start recall.
issues.fix_sketch: Track whether a default was supplied by spec arity (len(spec)>2) and forward an explicit None to the validator, so a None default reaches the handler as None instead of collapsing to ''.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_merge_memories (admin_handlers.py:1382) copies rows with a plain INSERT (no OR IGNORE) that omits project_id and only pre-skips rows with a non-empty msg_id; msg_id-less rows (the common do_store case) bypass dedup and collide with idx_memories_dedup_content whenever the target already holds identical (agent_id,'',channel,content), raising an uncaught IntegrityError that aborts the merge mid-loop -- defeating the documented strategy='skip' semantics -- and leaving prior INSERTs in the un-rolled-back shared transaction to be flushed by another handler's commit.
issues.pattern: INSERT INTO memories \(agent_id, msg_id, content, source, timestamp, metadata, channel\)
issues.fix_sketch: Use INSERT OR IGNORE (count skips via rowcount), preserve project_id, and wrap the merge loop in an explicit transaction with rollback on error.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: do_merge_memories copies rows with INSERT OR IGNORE (content collision -> counted skip, honouring strategy='skip'), preserves project_id, and wraps the merge in a transaction with rollback on error. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_merge_preserves_project_locked_and_skips_collision.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_migrate_channel_axis (maintenance_handlers.py:208-213 and :219) rewrites the channel column with two bare corpus-wide UPDATEs into the idx_memories_dedup_content UNIQUE index with no OR IGNORE and no try/except; if any recovered/globalized (agent_id,project_id,channel,content) equals an existing row, SQLite's default ABORT rolls back the whole statement (migrated=0), and because the collision is data-deterministic every re-run re-collides, so the knob2-flip channel migration can never complete on such a corpus.
issues.fix_sketch: Use UPDATE OR IGNORE (or a per-row try/except loop) so a single content collision skips that row and is reported, instead of aborting the whole migration.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_merge_memories source SELECT (admin_handlers.py:1366) omits the locked column and its INSERT never sets it, so every merged memory lands with locked=0; a memory the user explicitly locked loses its delete/edit protection after merge, and with mode='move' the original locked row is then removed by do_delete_agent_data's scope-wide DELETE, silently destroying the lock guarantee entirely.
issues.pattern: SELECT msg_id, content, source, timestamp, metadata, channel FROM memories WHERE agent_id = \?
issues.fix_sketch: Select `locked` in the source query and carry it through the INSERT column list so locked memories stay locked after merge.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: the merge source SELECT now reads project_id/channel/embedding/locked and the INSERT sets them, so merged memories keep their locked flag and γ-axes instead of landing unlocked in the global bucket. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_merge_preserves_project_locked_and_skips_collision.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_delete_memory deletes the DB row by id but computes the remote-vector removal namespace as `cpersona:{agent_id}` with agent_id defaulting to '' on the admin/dashboard path (agent_id not in the tool's required list), so admin_handlers.py:183 targets the empty namespace 'cpersona:' instead of the owner's `cpersona:{owner}` where do_store indexed it; under VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote the /remove is a no-op, the vector entry mem:{id} is orphaned permanently, and later recall wastes top-K slots on ghost hits that fail DB fetch, degrading recall. do_update_memory selects the owner and does this correctly.
issues.fix_sketch: SELECT the owner agent_id alongside locked and remove from cpersona:{owner} (mirror do_update_memory's row[1] handling) instead of the caller-supplied/empty agent_id.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: do_delete_memory reads the row's owner agent_id and builds the remote-vector namespace as cpersona:{owner_agent_id}, so a delete-by-id (no agent_id passed) removes the vector entry from the correct namespace instead of leaving it orphaned under 'cpersona:'.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_delete_memory (admin_handlers.py:173) and do_update_memory check the lock with a separate SELECT then issue an unguarded `DELETE/UPDATE ... WHERE id=? AND agent_id=?` with no `AND locked=0` folded into the DML; the statements straddle an await on the single shared aiosqlite connection, so over the concurrent HTTP transport a lock_memory committing locked=1 in the window is defeated and the now-locked memory is destroyed -- the same atomicity gap bug-007 fixed for the dedup DELETE, left uncovered in the admin path.
issues.pattern: WHERE id = \? AND agent_id = \? AND locked = 0
issues.fix_sketch: Fold `AND locked=0` into the DELETE/UPDATE DML and branch on cursor.rowcount so the lock check and mutation are one atomic statement.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_calibrate_threshold (admin_handlers.py:724) builds `vecs = np.array(vecs)` from raw np.frombuffer rows with no per-row dimension filter (same ragged construction in _temporal_adjacency_sims:431) and then does `vecs @ vecs.T`; on a mixed-embedding-dimension corpus (the jina-768d -> bge-1024d swap state the dim-change branch exists to handle) np.array of ragged vectors raises ValueError on numpy>=1.24, surfacing calibrate_threshold/set_recall_precision as an opaque {error} and, at ensure_calibrated_on_startup which is awaited before the transport try-block, able to abort server startup. The recall hot path already guards blob length; calibration does not.
issues.fix_sketch: Filter sampled rows to the modal/expected dimension (or detect length mismatch) and return ok:false with a clear 'mixed embedding dimensions' error instead of letting numpy raise.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) Every FTS backfill/rebuild migration step is gated `current < N and FTS_ENABLED` (database.py:254 etc.), so a DB first created with CPERSONA_FTS_ENABLED=false is stamped schema_version=13 with the non-FTS steps done; when FTS is later enabled, boot only runs the CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS FTS_SQL (empty virtual tables + triggers) and every version-gated rebuild is skipped, so all pre-existing memories/episodes are permanently absent from the FTS index and the keyword retriever silently returns zero hits for historical rows, dropping out of RRF/RSF/cascade with no error. Recoverable only via a manual check_health(fix=true) rebuild.
issues.fix_sketch: Track FTS-index-populated state separately from schema_version (or detect an empty *_fts over populated content) and run the ('rebuild')/backfill whenever FTS is enabled but the index is empty, regardless of current version.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: get_db() samples table-absence of memories_fts BEFORE creating FTS_SQL (fts_created_this_boot) — external-content FTS5 makes COUNT(*) mirror the content table, so table-absence is the only reliable empty-index probe. When the FTS index is created for the first time this boot (DB originally made with FTS off, now re-enabled), both FTS tables are backfilled via INSERT INTO x_fts(x_fts) VALUES('rebuild') after the migration ladder, so pre-existing memories/episodes become keyword-searchable again. Idempotent (only fires the boot the tables are created; a brand-new empty DB rebuilds two empty indexes) and non-fatal. Fix marker = fts_created_this_boot. Regression: test_followup_2_4_x.py::test_fts_backfill_when_enabled_after_created_disabled.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The remote branch of _search_vector (vector.py:147) hardcodes `"min_similarity": _get_vector_threshold(agent_id)` and ignores the min_similarity argument the local branch honours; _recall_rrf/_recall_rsf deliberately pass a lowered threshold (0.5x) so fusion has more candidates to rank, so under VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote (default recall mode rrf) that lowered threshold is discarded and the endpoint over-filters at the full threshold, returning a strictly smaller, differently-ranked candidate set than local for identical data.
issues.fix_sketch: Send `min_similarity if min_similarity is not None else _get_vector_threshold(agent_id)` in the remote POST, matching the local branch's effective_min_sim semantics.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The content-rewriting fixers check_memory_annotation (checks.py:86), check_discord_mention (:100) and check_oversized_content (:139) mutate memory content on fix=true but never re-embed the row; FTS stays consistent via the memories_fts_au trigger, but the vector embedding still encodes the OLD content and no fixer repairs it (check_null_embedding only touches NULL blobs, check_embedding_dimension only NULLs wrong-length blobs), so vector recall scores the row on stale semantics indefinitely -- the bug-011 failure class re-introduced in checks.py.
issues.pattern: UPDATE memories SET content = \? WHERE id = \?
issues.fix_sketch: After each content mutation, re-embed via vector._embedding_client + /index (as do_update_memory does post-bug-011), or set embedding=NULL so check_null_embedding re-embeds it.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) check_oversized_content truncates content via `UPDATE memories SET content = SUBSTR(content,1,?) WHERE id=?` (checks.py:139) with no locked=0 guard on the selection (:131) or the UPDATE, violating the documented bug-007 locked-row invariant; a memory longer than MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH that the user locked (reachable because import_memories inserts content un-sanitized) is irreversibly truncated during routine check_health(fix=true), also leaving a stale embedding.
issues.pattern: SUBSTR\(content, 1, \?\), embedding = NULL WHERE id = \? AND locked = 0
issues.fix_sketch: Add `AND locked = 0` to the oversized selection and UPDATE (and re-embed after truncation).
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: check_oversized_content's truncate UPDATE excludes locked rows (AND locked = 0), so a locked oversized memory keeps its full content. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_locked_rows_survive_destructive_maintenance.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) check_empty_content's fix DELETEs whitespace-only rows agent-scoped but without the `locked = 0` guard (checks.py:693) that its sibling check_duplicate_content applies and the module docstring promises; a locked memory whose content is whitespace-only (reachable via import_memories/merge_memories which insert content un-stripped) is destroyed by check_health(fix=true), breaking the bug-007 invariant.
issues.pattern: \(TRIM\(content\) = '' OR content IS NULL\) AND locked = 0
issues.fix_sketch: Add `AND locked = 0` to the empty_content DELETE.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: check_empty_content's DELETE excludes locked rows (AND locked = 0). Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_locked_rows_survive_destructive_maintenance.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) check_stale_pending_tasks counts (checks.py:657) and DELETEs (:664) rows in pending_memory_tasks with only `WHERE created_at < datetime('now','-1 hour')` and no agent_id predicate, though the table is per-agent and every sibling check applies _agent_scope; running check_health(agent_id='A', fix=true) deletes EVERY agent's >1h-old un-drained memory-write tasks, so another agent's stuck store is silently discarded and never persisted -- cross-agent data loss, same class as bug-007's scope leak in a distinct location.
issues.fix_sketch: Apply the _agent_scope clause to both the count and the DELETE so only the target agent's stale tasks are removed.
issues.fix_note: 2.5.0 (Task #180): the _agent_scope fragment was unified into isolation_where(); the pattern now pins the helper-derived {iso.and_clause} on the same statements.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_recall/do_recall_with_context take limit straight from the tool call and never clamp it (memory_handlers.py:803 `results = results[:limit]`, plus raw `LIMIT ?` in the FTS/no-query keyword paths), while _clamp_limit IS applied in do_list_memories/do_list_episodes; a negative limit reaches SQLite as `LIMIT -1` (unbounded), so recall materializes and scores the agent's entire corpus on the hot path and the trailing results[:-1] then silently drops rows -- an unbounded-scan/resource-exhaustion vector for a non-conforming client.
issues.fix_sketch: Call _clamp_limit(limit, CAP) at the top of do_recall/do_recall_with_context (mirroring the list handlers) before limit reaches SQL LIMIT or the slice.
issues.fix_note: 2.5.0 (Task #190) re-layered the cap: the library clamp ceiling moved from 100 to MAX_MEMORIES (the scan window) so full-depth library callers work, while the agent-facing 100 cap now lives in the recall tools' JSON Schema (maximum:100). The bug-032 threat (negative -> LIMIT -1 unbounded scan) stays closed by the same _clamp_limit floor.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The remote /search POST (vector.py:141) uses `_embedding_client._client.post` with no per-call timeout, inheriting the client's 30s DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECS on the recall hot path; the module defines PROBE_TIMEOUT_SECS=3.0 precisely so the probe does NOT inherit the 30s, but the actual recall query does -- so under VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote a hung/flapping endpoint blocks every recall ~30s before the except fires and falls back to local, collapsing throughput.
issues.fix_sketch: Pass an explicit short timeout (e.g. PROBE_TIMEOUT_SECS or a dedicated remote-search budget) to the /search POST so a hung endpoint falls back to local quickly.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: the remote /search POST passes an explicit timeout=REMOTE_SEARCH_TIMEOUT_SECS (config default 5.0s, CPERSONA_REMOTE_SEARCH_TIMEOUT_SECS) instead of inheriting the embed client's 30s DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECS, so a hung/flapping endpoint trips the except and falls back to local search in seconds. The buggy POST line legitimately remains (only the timeout kwarg is added), so the fix marker is the timeout kwarg, not the line's absence. Regression: test_followup_2_4_x.py::test_remote_search_falls_back_to_local_on_timeout plus test_v2438_hardening.py::test_remote_search_honors_min_similarity_argument (timeout assertion).

issues.summary: (LOW) The FTS-miss keyword fallback builds the LIKE pattern as f"%{query}%" (memory_handlers.py:1078) against `AND content LIKE ?` with no ESCAPE clause, so % and _ in the user query act as wildcards rather than literals (the adjacent source_id filter IS correctly escaped with _like_escape_prefix + ESCAPE); a query like 'a_b' or '50%' silently over-matches unrelated rows, polluting the keyword channel that feeds RRF fusion. Parameterized, so no SQL injection.
issues.fix_sketch: Escape the query with the source_id helper (escape backslash, %, _) and add ESCAPE to the content LIKE clause, mirroring the source_id path.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_recall_with_context filters/uses caller-supplied external_context via `e.get("content", "").strip()` (memory_handlers.py:872, and again at :888); the '' default only applies when the key is absent, so an entry with an explicit content=null (JSON null -> None) makes None.strip() raise AttributeError, aborting the whole recall_with_context call into an opaque {error} instead of skipping the one malformed entry (the tool schema declares no type constraint on content).
issues.fix_sketch: Coerce defensively to str: `(e.get('content') or '').strip()` at both call sites (:872 and :888).
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: both call sites in do_recall_with_context route external_context content through the null/type-safe _ctx_content() helper, which returns '' for a missing key, an explicit JSON null (None), a non-string value, or a non-dict entry, so a malformed entry is skipped instead of raising None.strip() and aborting the whole call into an opaque {error}. Fix marker = the _ctx_content helper (the buggy e.get("content","").strip() is gone). Regression: test_followup_2_4_x.py::test_recall_with_context_skips_null_content and ::test_recall_with_context_null_content_not_in_exclude.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_delete_agent_data (admin_handlers.py, DELETE at :305) wipes the agent's memories/profiles/episodes and remote vector namespace but never removes the agent's entries from vector._agent_thresholds/_agent_fused_gates/_agent_betas nor rewrites the calibration sidecar, so the stale per-agent threshold/beta survive in-process and are re-loaded on restart via _restore_calibration_state; a later same-id agent silently inherits calibration computed from the deleted corpus and under-/over-recalls until recalibration.
issues.fix_sketch: After the purge, pop agent_id from vector._agent_thresholds/_agent_fused_gates/_agent_betas and re-persist the calibration sidecar so no stale per-agent state survives the wipe.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_migrate_channel_axis relies on the invariant 'whatever is still discord is exactly the unrecoverable bucket' but executes the globalize `UPDATE memories SET channel='' WHERE channel='discord'` (maintenance_handlers.py:219) after the recoverable UPDATE with an await boundary between them on the shared connection; over the concurrent HTTP transport a do_store landing a new snowflake-session 'discord' row in that window is swept to channel='' instead of its concrete channel -- a silent channel-scope broadening (global-visibility) leak, not data loss.
issues.pattern: UPDATE memories SET channel = '' WHERE channel = 'discord'
issues.fix_sketch: Restrict the globalize UPDATE to genuinely-unrecoverable rows (WHERE channel='discord' AND NOT (session_id GLOB snowflake)) so a concurrently-inserted recoverable row is not swept into global.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_recall bumps recall_count/last_recalled_at and commits (memory_handlers.py:843) for every returned memory; the tool is registered readOnlyHint=true and is deliberately NOT one of the no_persist-gated write tools, yet those columns feed _compute_confidence's ranking, so a session put into no_persist mode for benchmarking/AB still permanently mutates confidence-ranking state (when CONFIDENCE_ENABLED) on every recall -- the exact contamination no_persist exists to prevent. Gated behind the default-off CONFIDENCE_ENABLED flag.
issues.pattern: if not deep and recall_counts and not no_persist\.is_paused\(\):
issues.fix_sketch: Skip the recall_count/last_recalled_at bump when no_persist.is_paused() (and reconsider readOnlyHint) so a no-persist session leaves ranking state unmutated.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: the recall_count/last_recalled_at bump is gated on `not no_persist.is_paused()`, so a no-persist (benchmark/AB) session no longer mutates confidence-ranking state on recall. The bump statement itself legitimately remains (normal sessions still bump), so the fix marker is the gate condition, not the statement's absence. Landed in ec91303 as the 11th task#171-approved fix; this registry flip was initially missed (the code + regression test shipped without it). Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_recall_does_not_bump_recall_count_under_no_persist.

issues.summary: (LOW) Every gamma-semantics assertion in test_isolation.py invokes the handler coroutines directly with project_id=None (e.g. :172), never through the ToolRegistry.auto_tool -> validate_str extraction the live MCP call uses, so the suite proves the handler's gamma math but gives false confidence that 'omit project_id -> all projects' works end-to-end; the omission->global-only wiring defect (bug-019) passes CI green because no test drives the registered tool handler with project_id absent.
issues.fix_sketch: Add a test that calls the registered tool handler (registry._handlers['recall']/'list_memories') with an arguments dict omitting project_id and asserts all-bucket rows are returned.

issues.summary: (HIGH) do_import_memories relies on rollback for all-or-nothing atomicity on a process-wide shared aiosqlite connection with no lock, so a concurrent commit (queue drain or do_recall) flushes its partial inserts and rollback cannot undo them.
issues.pattern: the whole restore runs inside one transaction
issues.fix_sketch: Do not depend on rollback for atomicity on a shared connection: give do_import_memories its own dedicated aiosqlite.connect() connection, or serialize all write handlers plus the queue drain behind one module-level asyncio.Lock so no other coroutine commits between the import's INSERTs and its final commit.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). During import loop await points, MemoryTaskQueue._drain or a concurrent do_recall/do_store commits on the same shared connection, flushing the import's partial rows; a later import error's rollback only reverts since the last commit, leaving a half-written corpus (violates bug-016 whole-restore contract). Reverse: import's rollback discards a drain's uncommitted episode INSERT while the drain still deletes its queue row, losing the episode.
issues.fix_note: do_import_memories originally held the shared write_lock() for the whole transaction (acquire/release + finally, 2.4.39). The 2.5.0 C-seam subsumed that fix: the whole restore now runs inside the transaction() context manager (lock + commit + auto-rollback owned by database.py); dry_run runs on the read seam. Marker = the conditional-seam entry in do_import_memories.

issues.summary: (HIGH) do_merge_memories claims whole-merge-in-one-transaction but shares the global aiosqlite connection with no lock, so a concurrent committer flushes its partial INSERTs mid-loop and its rollback-on-error cannot restore atomicity.
issues.pattern: holds the shared write lock for the whole merge
issues.fix_sketch: Isolate the merge transaction on a dedicated aiosqlite connection, or guard all write handlers plus the queue drain with one shared asyncio.Lock so no other coroutine can commit/rollback between the merge's INSERTs and its commit.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). The merge loop interleaves INSERT OR IGNOREs with await execute_fetchall dedup probes; at those await points a concurrent queue drain / do_recall / do_store commits on the shared connection, flushing the merge's already-inserted rows. A later merge error's rollback then leaves those rows behind (the half-merge outcome bug-020/022 aimed to prevent). Symmetrically the merge rollback can discard a drain's uncommitted episode INSERT while the drain deletes its queue row.
issues.fix_note: do_merge_memories originally held the shared write_lock() for the whole transaction (2.4.39). The 2.5.0 C-seam subsumed that fix: the whole merge now runs inside the transaction() context manager (lock + commit + auto-rollback owned by database.py); the move-mode delete still runs after the seam exits (no reentrancy). Marker = the bug-043 seam comment above the merge try block.

issues.summary: (HIGH) Local-mode vector search never applies the channel filter to episodes, so a channel-scoped recall leaks episode summaries from every other channel of the agent.
issues.pattern: WHERE agent_id = \? AND embedding IS NOT NULL\{proj_extra\}$
issues.fix_sketch: Mirror the memory branch: when channel is set, build a channel_clause (channel = ? OR channel = '') and splice it (with the matching param) into the episode SELECT, exactly as _search_episodes_fts does.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). Local mode (production default). Memory rows are channel-gated (channel = ? OR channel = '') and _search_episodes_fts gates episodes, but the vector episode fetch selects WHERE agent_id = ? AND embedding IS NOT NULL{proj_extra} with no channel predicate. An episode archived under channel roomA is cosine-scored and returned into a do_recall explicitly scoped to roomB — the exact cross-channel contamination the v2.4.22 channel axis exists to prevent, on the recall hot path.
issues.fix_note: Vector episode SELECT now applies (channel = ? OR channel = '') like the memory branch and _search_episodes_fts.

issues.summary: (HIGH) do_calibrate_threshold takes an unbounded caller-supplied sample_size and feeds it into both a LIMIT scan and an O(n^2) dense cosine matrix (vecs @ vecs.T), so a large value OOM-kills the whole MCP server process.
issues.fix_sketch: Clamp before the LIMIT/O(n^2) path: sample_n = min(sample_size or CALIBRATE_SAMPLE_SIZE, CALIBRATE_MAX_SAMPLE) with a config cap (~2000), mirroring the _clamp_limit discipline; the same cap must bound the _temporal_adjacency_sims call that receives the same value.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). sample_size is an exposed unclamped int param of the calibrate_threshold tool. calibrate_threshold(agent_id="", sample_size=20000) against a >=20000-row corpus loads 20000x768 float32 vectors then computes a 20000x20000 sim_matrix (~1.6 GB) plus triu_indices (~3 GB) and a copy on the separation path, OOM-killing the process and taking down recall for every agent on the shared connection. No upper bound exists at any layer.
issues.fix_note: Clamped calibrate_threshold sample_n to CALIBRATE_MAX_SAMPLE before the LIMIT/O(n^2) path, bounding the pairwise cosine matrix memory.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The recall_count/last_recalled_at bump treats episode result rows as memory ids, so recalling an episode increments the recall_count of an unrelated memory whose id collides with the episode id.
issues.pattern: returned_ids = \[r\.get\("id", -1\) for r in results if isinstance\(r\.get\("id"\), int\) and r\["id"\] > 0\]
issues.fix_sketch: Exclude episode rows from the bump: capture memory-only ids before the discriminators are popped (tag _search_episodes_fts rows with _rid=("ep", id) and keep only _rid[0]=="mem"), or skip rows whose source is {"System":"episode"} / content startswith "[Episode] ", then filter returned_ids against that set.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). With CONFIDENCE_ENABLED=true the bump runs. An episode result dict has a positive episode-table id and no discriminator (the _rid/_resolved markers are popped earlier), so returned_ids collects it and UPDATE memories SET recall_count = recall_count + 1 WHERE id IN (...) bumps the memory whose id equals the episode id. Both tables AUTOINCREMENT from 1 so collisions are the common case; low-id memories accrue phantom recall_count -> inflated confidence -> recall contamination, plus a falsified last_recalled_at.
issues.fix_note: Excluded episode rows from the recall_count bump via _is_episode_result (single-line comprehension replaced by a multi-line one that filters episodes).

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_import_memories dedups incoming memories by (agent_id, msg_id) only, dropping the project_id axis that do_store and the UNIQUE index (agent_id, project_id, msg_id) treat as part of identity, silently discarding a distinct cross-project memory.
issues.pattern: SELECT id FROM memories WHERE agent_id = \? AND msg_id = \? LIMIT 1
issues.fix_sketch: Add the project axis to the precheck: SELECT id FROM memories WHERE agent_id = ? AND project_id = ? AND msg_id = ? LIMIT 1 with record.get('project_id','') as a param, matching do_store and the UNIQUE index (or drop the precheck and rely on INSERT OR IGNORE).
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). do_store dedups project-scoped and the real constraint is idx_memories_dedup_msg_id ON memories(agent_id, project_id, msg_id) WHERE msg_id != ''. Importing msg_id='m1', project_id='beta' into an agent already holding msg_id='m1', project_id='alpha' makes this project-blind precheck find the alpha row and skip, so the distinct beta memory is never inserted even though INSERT OR IGNORE against the composite index would have accepted it — silent data loss on the backup/restore path.
issues.fix_note: Import (and merge, bug-047) dedup pre-check now includes project_id, matching do_store and idx_memories_dedup_msg_id.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) Remote-mode vector search fetches episode rows by id without the channel filter, leaking cross-channel episodes when VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote.
issues.fix_sketch: When channel is set, append AND (channel = ? OR channel = '') to this episode-by-id SELECT and pass channel in the params tuple, matching the memory-by-id branch.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). In the remote branch an ep:<id> hit is rehydrated via SELECT summary, start_time, resolved FROM episodes WHERE id = ?{iso_ep_fetch.and_clause} (2.5.0 Task #180 renamed the fragment from {proj_extra}; iso_ep_fetch deliberately still omits the channel axis, so the gap is unchanged). The by-id memory path adds (channel = ? OR channel = '') when channel is set, but this episode lookup omits it, so an episode from another channel is returned into a channel-scoped recall. Not the production default (production is local) but a real remote-mode correctness gap. The fix is one line now: build iso_ep_fetch with channel=channel.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_merge_memories dedups source memories against the target by (agent_id, msg_id) only, dropping project_id, so a distinct cross-project memory is skipped and lost during a merge.
issues.pattern: SELECT id FROM memories WHERE agent_id = \? AND msg_id = \? LIMIT 1
issues.fix_sketch: Include project_id in the target collision check: SELECT id FROM memories WHERE agent_id = ? AND project_id = ? AND msg_id = ? LIMIT 1, binding the source row's project_id, matching the project-scoped uniqueness the INSERT OR IGNORE enforces.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). The merge carries source project_id into the INSERT but the collision precheck is project-blind. Merging source A into target B where source has (msg_id='m1', project_id='') and B already has (msg_id='m1', project_id='X') matches on msg_id alone and skips the source's global-pool row, even though (B,'','m1') and (B,'X','m1') are distinct under the partial UNIQUE index and would coexist — silent loss of a source memory during agent consolidation.
issues.fix_note: Merge dedup pre-check now includes project_id (shared fix with bug-044).

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_archive_episode never pushes ep:{id} to the remote vector index, so in VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote episodes are permanently absent from semantic recall even though _search_vector's remote branch is written to consume ep: hits.
issues.pattern: INSERT INTO episodes \(agent_id, project_id, summary, keywords, start_time, end_time, embedding, resolved, channel\)
issues.fix_sketch: After the episode INSERT+commit, mirror the do_store remote sync: when VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=='remote' and a client/_http_url exist, POST to f'{base}/index' with namespace f'cpersona:{agent_id}' and items=[{'id': f'ep:{cursor.lastrowid}', 'text': summary}] in a non-fatal try/except (and add ep:{id} /remove in do_delete_episode for delete consistency).
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). With remote mode, archive_episode inserts the row with a local blob but never POSTs ep:<id> to {base}/index. A later recall's remote branch POSTs /search and returns only what the remote namespace holds; with no episodes indexed there are zero ep: hits and the branch returns without a local fallback, so every archived episode is unrecallable via semantic search in remote mode (FTS keyword recall survives). Distinct from the deferred bug-028: this is a write path with no push at all, unlike do_store/do_update_memory which do push.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The stdio-to-HTTP proxy forward loop only catches httpx.ConnectError and httpx.ReadTimeout; other transient httpx failures escape, terminate main(), and permanently kill the MCP bridge for the whole session.
issues.fix_sketch: Broaden to except (httpx.TransportError, httpx.HTTPError) as e: (or at least add httpx.TimeoutException) in a single handler that calls _write_error(line, ...) and continue, so any transient transport failure reports a JSON-RPC error for that request and keeps the loop alive.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). In remote deployment, client.post() can raise httpx.ConnectTimeout/PoolTimeout/WriteTimeout (subclasses of TimeoutException, not ConnectError) or httpx.RemoteProtocolError (ProtocolError, not ConnectError/ReadTimeout). Neither except clause matches, so the exception propagates out of the while True loop and out of main(); asyncio.run() re-raises and the proxy process exits. The dequeued request gets no JSON-RPC error and every subsequent memory tool call in the session fails because the stdio bridge is dead — a transient blip becomes a permanent session-wide outage.
issues.fix_note: Broadened the proxy forward-loop except to (httpx.TransportError, httpx.HTTPError) so a transient timeout/protocol error no longer kills the whole bridge.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The recall_count/last_recalled_at bookkeeping UPDATE at the end of do_recall is unguarded, so a failure of this non-essential write sinks the entire already-computed recall response.
issues.fix_sketch: Wrap the returned_ids UPDATE+commit in try/except that logs a warning and continues, so a ranking-bookkeeping write failure never fails the recall read itself.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). do_recall finishes fusion/scoring/gating with messages ready, then unconditionally runs an unwrapped UPDATE memories SET recall_count = ... WHERE id IN (...) + commit. If that write raises (sqlite3.OperationalError 'database is locked' from a second writer under WAL, or disk-full/I-O error) the exception propagates and the fully-computed recall result is discarded — the read fails entirely, inconsistent with recall being readOnlyHint=true and every remote index push being wrapped non-fatal.
issues.fix_note: Wrapped the recall_count/last_recalled_at bump in try/except (non-fatal) so a bookkeeping write failure no longer discards the recall result.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) export_memories is declared readOnlyHint=True but do_export_memories writes/overwrites an arbitrary caller-controlled filesystem path, so a host that auto-approves read-only tools performs an unconfirmed destructive file write.
issues.fix_sketch: Drop readOnlyHint=True for export_memories: set annotations=ToolAnnotations(readOnlyHint=False, destructiveHint=True) so hosts require confirmation. Additionally confine output_path in do_export_memories (reject absolute paths / '..' traversal, or resolve under a configured export dir and verify realpath stays within it) before os.makedirs/open.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). When a host treats readOnlyHint=True as safe to auto-execute (the hint's purpose), a prompt-injected export_memories(agent_id="x", output_path="/Users/hachiya/.claude/settings.json") runs os.makedirs(dirname) then open(output_path,"w") and truncates/overwrites that existing file with JSONL export content — an environment-modifying destructive write with no confirmation because the tool is mislabeled read-only. Any reachable path (config files, cron dirs, dotfiles) can be clobbered. Line 692 is the export_memories annotation among several readOnlyHint=True lines.
issues.fix_note: export_memories annotation is now readOnlyHint=False/destructiveHint=True (a host confirms the write); do_export_memories also confines output_path via _confine_export_path.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) check_missing_profile ignores the requested agent_id and reports missing-profile findings for ALL agents, so an agent-scoped check_health misreports its subject's health.
issues.fix_sketch: Apply the agent scope like every sibling check: add WHERE m.agent_id = ? (and pass agent_id) when agent_id is non-empty, mirroring _agent_scope, so the finding is limited to the requested agent.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). Agent A has a profile; agent B does not. check_health(agent_id="A") runs a corpus-wide LEFT JOIN with no agent filter, returning {type:missing_profile, agents:["B"]}. do_check_health then reports healthy=False and injects an issue about B into A's issues list and severity_summary even though A is healthy — the same scope-leak class the bug-031 comment says every per-agent check must avoid.
issues.fix_note: check_missing_profile now scopes to the requested agent (m.agent_id = ?), so an agent-scoped check no longer reports another agent's missing profile.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_import_memories dry_run over-reports imported_memories because the content-UNIQUE-index collision skip only runs in the non-dry-run branch.
issues.pattern: SELECT 1 FROM memories WHERE agent_id = \? AND project_id = \? AND channel = \? AND content = \? LIMIT 1
issues.fix_sketch: In dry_run, replicate the content-uniqueness check before counting (SELECT 1 FROM memories WHERE agent_id=? AND project_id=? AND channel=? AND content=? LIMIT 1, count as skipped when present) so previewed imported/skipped counts match a real run.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). Import a JSONL memory with content C and empty/absent msg_id where the target already holds (agent_id, project_id, channel, C). The msg_id probe does not fire, so dry_run falls straight to imported_memories += 1, while a real run hits INSERT OR IGNORE -> rowcount 0 -> skipped_memories += 1; continue. The preview reports N imports where the actual run imports fewer, defeating dry_run's stated preview purpose.
issues.fix_note: do_import_memories dry_run now replicates the content-uniqueness collision skip so previewed imported/skipped counts match a real run (shared probe with bug-057).

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_merge_memories dry_run over-reports merged_memories: the content-UNIQUE-index skip (INSERT OR IGNORE rowcount==0) only happens in the non-dry-run branch.
issues.pattern: SELECT 1 FROM memories WHERE agent_id = \? AND project_id = \? AND channel = \? AND content = \? LIMIT 1
issues.fix_sketch: Mirror the content-uniqueness collision check inside the dry_run path (count such rows as skipped_memories) so preview counts equal the real merge counts.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). Merge source into target where a source memory's content already exists in target under a different/empty msg_id. The msg_id probe does not skip them, so dry_run reaches merged_memories += 1 for each, while a real run's INSERT OR IGNORE returns rowcount 0 -> skipped_memories += 1; continue. dry_run previews more merges than actually occur, contradicting its documented accurate-preview contract.
issues.fix_note: do_merge_memories dry_run now replicates the content-uniqueness collision skip (shared probe with bug-056).

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The bug-026 first-boot FTS backfill uses table-existence as its only done-sentinel and swallows rebuild failures, so a transient 'rebuild' error permanently leaves historical rows unindexed with no retry.
issues.fix_sketch: Make backfill completion durable rather than inferred from table presence: on rebuild failure re-raise into migration_error so the version stamp is withheld and the next boot retries, or record a separate fts_backfilled marker and gate the backfill on that marker; also set PRAGMA busy_timeout at connect so a concurrent-writer BUSY does not fail the first attempt.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). Re-enabling FTS on an existing DB creates memories_fts/episodes_fts (flipping the future fts_created_this_boot sentinel False). If the backfill hits SQLITE_BUSY (WAL, no busy_timeout PRAGMA set) or a transient I/O error, the except swallows it non-fatally; because the migration ladder succeeded, schema_version is stamped and no later boot re-backfills (tables now exist), so the FTS index stays empty forever for all pre-existing rows — keyword retrieval silently returns zero hits for historical data with no surfaced error. Distinct from the already-fixed bug-026 (ordering/one-shot) — this is the durability/error-swallow gap.
issues.fix_note: FTS backfill completion is now durable (PRAGMA user_version bit, set on failure/retried next boot) instead of inferred from table presence; a busy_timeout avoids first-attempt BUSY.

issues.summary: (LOW) recall_counts is built from all positive-id result rows including episodes, so an episode whose id collides with a memory id inherits that memory's recall_count/last_recalled_at when computing the episode's confidence score.
issues.pattern: mem_ids = \[r\["id"\] for r in results if isinstance\(r\.get\("id"\), int\) and r\["id"\] > 0\]
issues.fix_sketch: Restrict mem_ids to memory rows only (same discriminator as bug-040: _rid[0]=='mem', or exclude source=={"System":"episode"}) so episode ids never key into the memories recall_count map.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). With CONFIDENCE_ENABLED=true, mem_ids includes episode rows (positive int id). The SELECT id, recall_count, last_recalled_at FROM memories WHERE id IN (...) matches the colliding memory row, so recall_counts[episode_id] = that memory's counts, feeding the episode's _compute_confidence an unrelated memory's recall_count and last_recalled_at -> wrong recall-boost/recency-penalty and mis-ranking of episode results. Same collision root cause as bug-040 but on the read/scoring side.
issues.fix_note: Restricted mem_ids to memory rows only via _is_episode_result so an episode id never keys into the memories recall_count map.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_merge_memories gates on no-persist before checking dry_run, so a read-only dry_run=True merge preview is short-circuited into a fabricated no-op instead of returning real preview counts.
issues.fix_sketch: Change the gate to if no_persist.is_paused() and not dry_run: so the write-free preview path runs normally under pause and only the actual mutating merge (dry_run=False) is skipped.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). During a paused session, merge_memories(..., dry_run=True) is write-free (every mutation is guarded by if not dry_run), yet the gate at 1467 returns make_skipped_response with merged/skipped:0/persisted:false. The operator receives all-zero preview counts masking what a real merge would do, contradicting the 'read tools unaffected' no-persist contract. Line 1467 is the do_merge_memories occurrence of the is_paused gate.
issues.fix_note: do_merge_memories only gates the write path on no-persist; a write-free dry_run=True preview now runs normally under pause.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_import_memories (and do_merge_memories) insert memory/episode rows directly without any remote /index push, so imported/merged rows are invisible to remote-mode vector recall.
issues.fix_sketch: After a successful non-dry-run import/merge insert, when VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=='remote' collect new (id, content/summary) pairs and POST them to f'{base}/index' under cpersona:{target_agent_id} (batched, non-fatal try/except), matching the do_store id scheme (mem:{id} / ep:{id}).
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). With VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote, import_memories (dashboard restore) and merge_memories (agent consolidation) insert rows for target_agent_id but never POST them to {base}/index under cpersona:{target_agent_id}. Because the remote /search branch returns early on success with no local fallback, restored/merged rows never appear in recall until an unrelated re-index (there is none for import/merge). Distinct from deferred bug-028 (check_null_embedding re-push): these are separate write handlers that, unlike do_store/do_update_memory, sync nothing. Line 1350 is the import INSERT (1524 the merge INSERT).

issues.summary: (LOW) In do_check_health stats, memories is agent-scoped when agent_id is given but episodes and profiles are always corpus-wide, so the same stats block mixes scopes under unprefixed keys.
issues.fix_sketch: When agent_id is set, scope episodes/profiles too (add WHERE agent_id = ?), or move the corpus-wide totals under explicitly-named keys (global_episodes/global_profiles) so scoping is consistent with memories.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). Agent A has 10 memories/2 episodes; agent B has 100/50. check_health(agent_id="A") returns stats.memories=10 (agent-scoped) but stats.episodes=52 and stats.profiles=all-agents (no agent filter). A dashboard reading stats.episodes for the scoped agent shows 52 instead of 2 while stats.memories does not — inconsistent scoping under identically-named keys.
issues.fix_note: check_health stats now scope episodes/profiles/pending_tasks to the requested agent, consistent with memories.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_check_health reports healthy/severity_summary from issues FOUND, not issues REMAINING, so a fix=True run that fully repairs the DB still reports healthy=False (and the checkup CLI exits nonzero after a successful repair).
issues.fix_sketch: After a fix run, re-derive healthy/severity_summary from unresolved issues only (exclude entries where issue.get('fixed') is True), or re-run the affected checks post-commit to report residual state, so a successful auto-repair is not reported as unhealthy/gating.
issues.note: v2.4.39 audit (task#150-style multi-finder x adversarial verify). A DB whose only defect is a fixable critical: check_health(fix=True) repairs it and stamps issue['fixed']=True but still returns the issue, so run_health_checks counts severity_summary['critical']=1 and do_check_health sets healthy=False. Via python -m cpersona.checkup --fix, exit_code(severity_summary) returns 2 ('critical always gates') even though the repair succeeded, so a repair cron/CI reads exit 2 = failure after a clean fix.
issues.fix_note: After a fix run, healthy/severity_summary are re-derived from a read-only fix=False re-run (residual state), so a successful auto-repair is not reported unhealthy/gating.

issues.summary: (HIGH) do_calibrate_threshold / _temporal_adjacency_sims decode every stored embedding with np.frombuffer(blob, float32) with no length guard; a blob whose byte length is not a multiple of 4 (planted via a truncated/crafted import_memories embedding_b64) raises ValueError. ensure_calibrated_on_startup calls calibrate with no try/except before the transport starts, so one poison row crashes the whole server at boot for every agent.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: _decode_embedding rejects a decoded blob whose length is not a multiple of 4 at ingestion; _safe_frombuffer(blob) returns None for a corrupt blob and the two consumption loops skip it instead of crashing. Fix marker = _safe_frombuffer.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) check_health stats.axes calls axis_distribution(db) with no agent_id (the axes sibling bug-058 missed), so a per-agent health run returns a corpus-wide project_id/channel distribution — check_health(agent_id='A') on the shared multi-agent DB discloses every other agent's bucket names + counts.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: axis_distribution(db, agent_id='') scopes both axis queries via _agent_scope(agent_id); do_check_health passes agent_id. Empty agent_id keeps the corpus-wide CLI view. Fix marker = axis_distribution(db, agent_id.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The stdio proxy never inspects response.status_code; httpx does not raise for 4xx/5xx, so a non-2xx non-SSE body (502 HTML, 401 JSON, 500 page) is written verbatim to stdout as a JSON-RPC message, desyncing the client's line reader and leaving the request unanswered until timeout.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: after session-id tracking, a status_code >= 400 emits a proper id-keyed JSON-RPC error (like the bug-051 transport path) and continues, instead of forwarding the body. Fix marker = response.status_code >= 400. [2026-07-08] Guard superseded by bug-082: >= 400 widened to reject everything outside 2xx (3xx redirects carried the same stream-corruption class). Fix marker updated to the 2xx guard.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) archive_episode is registered idempotentHint=True but do_archive_episode does a bare INSERT with no OR IGNORE / unique constraint, so every call appends a new episode. A host retrying after a lost response (trusting the idempotency hint) double-stores the episode, inflating recall and list_episodes.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: archive_episode annotation set idempotentHint=False (honest: not retry-safe). Fix marker = bug-064.

issues.summary: (LOW) get_recall_precision is a pure read (never persists) but is registered with no annotations, so it lacks readOnlyHint=True unlike every peer read tool; a host that auto-approves reads needlessly prompts for it.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: get_recall_precision registered with ToolAnnotations(readOnlyHint=True), consistent with get_profile/list_memories/etc. Fix marker = bug-065.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_calibrate_threshold passes caller-supplied percentile/z_factor to numpy unvalidated (bug-053 clamped sample_size but left these). np.quantile requires q in [0,1]; a natural 95-vs-0.95 confusion raises an opaque ValueError.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: percentile >1 is interpreted as a percent (/100), then validated to (0,1] with a clear error; z_factor clamped to [-10,10]. Fix marker = 'percentile must be in'.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The bug-060 FTS-backfill-pending flag is armed only reactively (in the rebuild except) and written in the same uncommitted transaction as the rebuild. executescript(FTS_SQL) implicitly commits the empty FTS tables first, so a process kill between that and the final commit leaves the tables present + the pending bit clear — the next boot never re-indexes historical rows (permanent silent FTS desync).
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: the pending bit is ARMED and committed BEFORE the FTS tables are created whenever a backfill is needed; cleared+committed only after a successful rebuild. A killed boot leaves bit=1 so the next boot retries. Fix marker = bug-067.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_migrate_channel_axis holds write_lock across its UPDATEs + commit with no try/except/rollback, unlike import/merge. If the globalize UPDATE or the commit raises after the first UPDATE, the async-with releases the lock without rollback, leaving a partial write for the next committer to flush (the bug-042/043 class).
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: the migrate write block is wrapped in try/except that rolls back and re-raises on any fault, matching import/merge. Fix marker = bug-068.

issues.summary: (LOW) check_fts_integrity's fix-path re-verify uses the enhanced INSERT ...(fts, rank) VALUES('integrity-check', 1) unconditionally; on SQLite < 3.42 that raises OperationalError (unsupported), caught as DatabaseError and reported fixed:False even though the rebuild succeeded.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: the re-verify mirrors the detection enhanced→structural fallback ladder; only a genuine DatabaseError after rebuild marks it unfixed. Fix marker = bug-069.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_import_memories dry_run over-counts intra-file duplicate memories: dry_run performs no INSERT OR IGNORE, so a duplicate later in the same file (same msg_id, or same content on empty msg_id) is counted as imported, while a real run sees its own uncommitted row on the shared connection and skips it. bug-056's probe queries only the persistent DB.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: dry_run tracks within-file identities on both dedup axes (seen_msgid / seen_content) so a repeat in the same file is previewed as skipped. Fix marker = seen_content.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_merge_memories dry_run over-counts intra-batch episodes with duplicate summary: episodes have no summary uniqueness constraint and use a bare INSERT, so real-run intra-batch dedup relies on the existing-check seeing uncommitted rows; dry_run inserts nothing so two source episodes with the same summary both count as merged.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: dry_run tracks within-batch summaries (seen_summary) scoped to the target agent. Fix marker = seen_summary.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_check_health(fix=True) wraps the whole run_health_checks in the shared write_lock; check_null_embedding / check_null_episode_embedding then do up to ~1000 sequential embedding HTTP calls under the lock, stalling every writer (do_store, queue drain, import/merge) for the entire re-embed (up to 30s/row).
issues.fix_note: v2.4.39: do_check_health pre-computes the re-embeddings via prefetch_null_embeddings OUTSIDE the lock; the checks apply cached blobs (live-embed fallback on cache miss / CLI). The lock now covers only the DB writes+commit. Fix marker = prefetch_null_embeddings.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_export_memories loads whole tables via execute_fetchall (memories/episodes/profiles, no LIMIT) plus base64 embedding copies, materialising the full corpus in RAM before writing the JSONL — OOM risk on large exports (agent_id='' spans every agent). Deferred: streaming via cursor is a mechanical follow-up.

(LOW) _is_episode_result classifies episode-vs-memory partly by content.startswith('[Episode] '); a user memory whose content legitimately starts with that literal is misclassified and permanently excluded from the recall_count bump / confidence join. Deferred: tag FTS episode rows with an explicit _rid=('ep', id) at the source to remove the heuristic.

(MEDIUM, remote-mode) The remote vector episode fetch omits the channel predicate bug-045 added to the local branch, so a channel-scoped recall in VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote can return an episode from another channel (cross-channel contamination). Deferred with the remote-mode trio bug-046/049/050 (production-moot: current deployment is local vector mode).
issues.pattern: SELECT summary, start_time, resolved FROM episodes WHERE id = \?

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_merge_memories episode dedup probe matched (agent_id, summary) only, omitting the project_id/channel isolation axes — a distinct cross-project episode with the same summary text was skipped, and mode='move' then deleted it with the source agent (permanent data loss). Episode analog of the fixed memory bug-047.
issues.pattern: SELECT id FROM episodes WHERE agent_id = \? AND summary = \? LIMIT 1
issues.note: Round-3 audit of the frozen 2.4.39 baseline (125b33b), 12-finder x 3-lens adversarial verify. Episodes have no uniqueness constraint, so the summary-only pre-check was the ONLY dedup gate before a bare INSERT. The dry_run seen_summary key was equally summary-only (bug-071 parity broken for the cross-bucket case).
issues.fix_note: Probe now binds (agent_id, project_id, channel, summary), matching the source row's axes; seen_summary carries the same key. Class gate: test_structural_gates.py::test_identity_probes_carry_isolation_axes. Regression: test_audit_r3.py.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The bug-072 prefetch cache was keyed by row id only and applied without checking that the row's content was unchanged since the (unlocked) prefetch — a raced do_update_memory (embed-failure path) or a sibling fixer rewrite (memory_annotation/discord_mention/oversized_content) left the row NULL with NEW text, and the locked re-embed stamped the OLD text's vector onto it: a silent content/embedding desync (the bug-028 coherence class), undetectable afterwards.
issues.fix_note: prefetch_null_embeddings now caches (text, blob); every cached write goes through a conditional UPDATE ... WHERE id = ? AND embedding IS NULL AND {text_col} = ? so a stale blob can never attach to changed content (revalidated atomically at write time). Fix marker = the conditional UPDATE predicate. Regression: test_audit_r3.py + the structural-gate coherence asserts.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) merge_memories was annotated destructiveHint=False although mode='move' ends in do_delete_agent_data(source) — the same irreversible whole-agent wipe the delete_agent_data tool declares destructiveHint=True for. A host/kernel HITL approval gate keyed on the hint (vendored mcp_utils contract) was silently bypassed via the merge path. Annotation-truthfulness class (bug-054/064).
issues.fix_note: merge_memories now declares destructiveHint=True (annotations must reflect the worst reachable behavior). Fix marker = the bug-078 comment at the registration site. Regression: test_audit_r3.py::test_merge_memories_declares_destructive.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_import_memories short-circuited a read-only dry_run preview under no-persist into a fabricated all-zero response — the unfixed import twin of bug-048 (which fixed merge only). A dry_run import is entirely write-free (every INSERT/UPSERT, commit, and the write_lock acquire are guarded by `if not dry_run`), so gating it violated the 'read tools unaffected by no-persist' contract and masked real would-import counts.
issues.fix_note: Gate is now `no_persist.is_paused() and not dry_run`, byte-parallel to the bug-048 merge fix. Fix marker = the bug-079 comment (the gate expression itself also exists at the merge site, so the comment disambiguates). Regression: test_audit_r3.py.

issues.summary: (LOW) The local vector episode retriever dropped ALL episodes whenever source_id was set, ignoring the channel-scope override that the three FTS recall drivers and the do_recall docstring contract honor (`not source_id or channel` — the session-start grounding path). Semantic episode recall was silently defeated on exactly that grounding path; over-restriction (safe direction), no leak.
issues.fix_note: Local branch gate is now `src_like and not channel` — with a channel filter the bug-045 channel clause scopes the fetch, so episodes are safe to return. The REMOTE branch deliberately keeps the conservative all-drop gate until the remote fetch carries the channel predicate (deferred bug-075). Regression: test_audit_r3.py::test_vector_episodes_returned_with_source_id_plus_channel.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) A negative sample_size bypassed the bug-053 calibration clamp: min() bounds only the upper side, a negative value is truthy through the `or` default, and SQLite treats LIMIT -1 as UNBOUNDED — the whole corpus flowed into the O(n^2) similarity matrix, reopening the OOM DoS bug-053 fixed (and unbounding _temporal_adjacency_sims' LIMIT too).
issues.fix_note: Two-sided clamp: sample_n = max(1, min(sample_size or CALIBRATE_SAMPLE_SIZE, CALIBRATE_MAX_SAMPLE)) — mirrors the two-sided guards percentile/z_factor already had (bug-066). Regression: test_audit_r3.py::test_calibrate_negative_sample_size_is_clamped.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The bug-063 proxy status guard only rejected >= 400: the client is built without follow_redirects, so a reverse proxy's 301/302/307/308 (http->https or /mcp trailing-slash canonicalization) surfaced with an HTML body, bypassed the guard, and was written verbatim to stdout — the same JSON-RPC stream desync bug-063 targeted, from the redirect range (multi-line body desyncs the reader session-wide; an empty body hangs the request).
issues.fix_note: Guard now rejects everything outside 2xx (only a 2xx can carry a JSON-RPC/SSE payload), surfacing an id-keyed JSON-RPC error and keeping the loop alive. No behavioural proxy suite exists (bug-051/063 precedent) — the fix marker pins the guard.

issues.summary: (LOW) bug-072 residuals: check_embedding_dimension probed embed(['test']) INSIDE maybe_write_lock(fix) on every fix run; _reembed_null_rows fell back to LIVE embeds under the lock on any cache miss; and rows NULLed DURING the locked run (dimension-mismatch NULLing, annotation/mention/oversize rewrites) were absent from prefetch by construction — up to 500+500 sequential HTTP round-trips while holding the shared write lock, the exact cross-writer stall bug-072 was created to remove.
issues.fix_note: Three-part fix: (1) the dimension probe runs in the unlocked prefetch phase (probe_embedding_dim -> embedding_cache['expected_dim']); (2) the locked re-embed never live-embeds when a cache dict is present (cache misses stay NULL); (3) do_check_health runs a second UNLOCKED prefetch pass after the fix run and applies it under a short lock with content revalidated in the UPDATE (bug-077 guard) — single-run convergence without HTTP under the lock. Class gate: test_structural_gates.py::test_check_health_never_embeds_under_write_lock.

issues.summary: (LOW) bug-040/041 residual: the fix excluded episode rows from recall_counts' CONSTRUCTION but both LOOKUP sites still keyed by raw row id — episode #N co-recalled with memory #N inherited the memory's (recall_count, last_recalled_at), inflating the episode's confidence score (ranking + reported value). Gated behind CONFIDENCE_ENABLED (default off), so default deployments were unaffected.
issues.fix_note: Both lookup sites (ranking loop in _apply_recall_scoring and the response confidence loop) now short-circuit episodes to (0, '') — closing the lookup side of the invariant the bug-041 docstring states ('an episode id must never key into a memories query'). Regression: test_audit_r3.py::test_episode_confidence_ignores_colliding_memory_recall_count.

issues.summary: (HIGH) Vector scan window was derived from the response limit: _search_vector fetched only the newest min(MAX_MEMORIES, max(limit*10, 100)) rows before cosine ranking, so a default limit=10 recall ranked only the newest 100 rows and anything older was structurally invisible to the vector retriever. The 2.4.38 limit clamp (bug-032) then closed the only escape hatch (callers passing a huge limit), collapsing LMEB LongMemEval from ~78 to 38.68 on MiniLM; re-running the harness with the clamp bypassed restored 76.99, confirming the window as the sole cause. The MAX_MEMORIES=500 default additionally capped reach below real corpus sizes even for unclamped callers.
issues.fix_note: Scan breadth decoupled from response size: _search_vector now fetches and ranks the newest MAX_MEMORIES rows regardless of the caller's limit, and the MAX_MEMORIES default is raised 500 -> 10000 (CPERSONA_MAX_MEMORIES env for larger corpora, e.g. benchmarks). The bug-032 response clamp is unchanged and correct.
