The LIKE fallback needs no change: its LIMIT applies after the content predicate, capping matched rows rather than scanned rows, so old rows were always reachable there. test_scan_window.py pins both contracts (window decoupling, 10000 default, LIKE reachability).

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) Dirty reads on the shared connection: connection() yielded the writer's own aiosqlite connection, and WAL snapshot isolation only exists ACROSS connections — a SELECT executed inside another coroutine's open transaction observed its uncommitted rows. do_store's dedup probe could match an import row that was later rolled back (the acknowledged store then existed nowhere: silent loss), and recall/get_contents could serve phantom rows. Found by the 2.5.0b1 hybrid audit (Anthropic concurrency finder), mechanism self-verified.
issues.fix_note: Read seam moved to a dedicated read connection (_get_read_db), re-keyed on the current write connection after a reboot, with any implicit transaction rolled back on scope exit (FTS5's integrity-check is spelled as an INSERT and would otherwise pin the WAL snapshot forever). Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_read_seam_does_not_see_uncommitted_writes / _unpins_snapshot_on_scope_exit / _rekeys_after_reboot.

issues.summary: (LOW, latent) get_db() memoised the connection before schema/migrations completed: a failed boot left _db set, so every later caller took the fast path and received a half-initialised connection; concurrent first-touch callers could also run the committing migration ladder twice. Unreachable in the production boot order (init_db is awaited before the transport starts).
issues.fix_note: The global is published only after _init_schema succeeds, under _init_lock; a failed boot closes the connection and leaves _db unset so the retry re-runs the idempotent ladder. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_get_db_is_not_memoized_on_failed_init.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) merge_memories(mode='move') ran the copy and the source wipe as two separate locked transactions. Any writer queued behind the merge on the FIFO write lock could insert into the source between the merge commit and the delete — do_delete_agent_data then wiped an acknowledged row that was never copied (deterministic loss, not just a crash window). A crash between the transactions degraded the move to a copy, and a no-persist pause landing in between silently skipped the wipe under ok:true.
issues.fix_note: The wipe runs INSIDE the merge transaction via the _delete_agent_rows leaf; only the non-DB side effects (calibration purge, remote namespace purge) run post-commit. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_merge_move_is_one_atomic_unit / _deletes_source_in_same_call.

issues.summary: (LOW, vestigial surface) The queue drain committed do_archive_episode's INSERT and _delete_task's DELETE as two transactions: a crash (or a failure of the delete alone) replayed the bare, non-idempotent INSERT on the next drain and duplicated the episode. No production path enqueues today (bug-006 removed the last one), so only crash-recovered legacy rows hit this.
issues.fix_note: do_archive_episode is split into _prepare_episode_row (validation + embedding HTTP, outside the lock) and the _insert_episode_row leaf; the drain runs the episode INSERT and the task-row DELETE in ONE transaction. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_drain_archive_is_single_transaction / _archives_and_deletes_task_atomically; FIFO dispatch re-pinned in test_task_queue.py::test_fifo_ordering.

issues.summary: (LOW) The drain distinguished only raise-vs-return: a handler-returned {ok:false} dict (e.g. a crash-recovered legacy archive task with no summary) was deleted and logged 'completed' — silently discarding exactly the rows the crash-recovery queue exists to preserve.
issues.fix_note: A failure-shaped return routes into the retry/discard path like a raise, so legacy summary-less rows fail visibly (error log + max-retries discard) instead of masquerading as successes. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_drain_treats_failure_dict_as_failure.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_export_memories opened the destination with open(path, 'w'), destroying the previous backup the moment the export started; a mid-export fault (disk full, kill) left a truncated JSONL that do_import_memories restored 'successfully' (silent partial restore of the disaster-recovery tool). The pre-write COUNT queries could also disagree with the later row SELECTs (torn header). Found by the 2.5.0b1 hybrid audit (Anthropic fault finder).
issues.fix_note: Export writes to a temp file and os.replace()s on success; the header is derived from the fetched rows so it always matches the body; import validates the body against the header and rejects truncated files with a full rollback. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_export_failure_preserves_previous_backup / test_import_rejects_truncated_file.

issues.summary: (LOW) Export omitted recall_count / last_recalled_at entirely and import re-stamped created_at with import time: a backup round-trip zeroed the recall-frequency scoring boost (utils.py raw_boost) and reordered the vector scan window / episode session probes that ORDER BY created_at.
issues.fix_note: All three columns ride the export record and the import INSERT (created_at via COALESCE for older files). Merge's SELECT/INSERT carries them via the same audit batch. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_roundtrip_preserves_recall_stats_and_created_at.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_delete_agent_data left the agent's pending_memory_tasks rows in place: a crash-recovered task for the wiped agent would re-INSERT episode/profile rows, silently resurrecting data for a deleted agent.
issues.fix_note: _delete_agent_rows clears pending_memory_tasks in the same transaction as the other tables and reports deleted_pending_tasks. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_delete_agent_data_purges_pending_tasks.

issues.summary: (LOW) Episode keywords were bound to db.execute untouched: a JSON-array value (the natural hand-authored format) reached sqlite3 as a Python list -> InterfaceError -> the whole import aborted, while the same file previewed cleanly under dry_run because the binding lived on the write path only (type-validation parity gap, distinct from the count-parity class bug-056/070/071).
issues.fix_note: Field extraction/coercion happens on BOTH paths before the dry_run branch; arrays are joined to the documented space-separated string. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_import_keywords_array_is_coerced.

issues.summary: (LOW) _save_calibration_state wrote the sidecar in place (open 'w' + json.dump) and swallowed OSError with a log line: a mid-write kill corrupted the sidecar for EVERY agent (loader returns None; with auto-calibration off, all agents silently fall back to the global default threshold), and do_calibrate_threshold returned ok:true even when persistence failed.
issues.fix_note: Sidecar written via temp + os.replace; the save returns success and calibrate_threshold surfaces it as sidecar_persisted. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_sidecar_save_failure_returns_false.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_set_recall_precision applied the in-memory beta override before calling do_calibrate_threshold and rolled it back only on an ok:false RETURN — a RAISED calibration failure (transient DB lock, numpy fault) leaked the un-persisted beta into process memory, and the next routine calibration persisted a gate at a beta the caller was told failed to apply.
issues.fix_note: The calibrate call is wrapped so a raise takes the same rollback path as ok:false. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_set_recall_precision_rolls_back_beta_on_raise.

issues.summary: (LOW) duplicate_content ran before oversized_content in HEALTH_CHECKS: the oversized fixer truncates content to MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH and can mint fresh duplicate groups after the dup check already passed. The bug-059 residual re-run reported them (not silent), but a single fix pass could not converge.
issues.fix_note: oversized_content (and the other content rewriters, which already preceded) run before duplicate_content. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_health_checks_order_oversized_before_duplicate.

issues.summary: (HIGH) Five fixers mutated rows WITHOUT the locked = 0 guard: check_invalid_json (source/metadata -> '{}'), check_invalid_timestamp (timestamp -> created_at), check_timestamp_format_drift (timestamp normalisation), check_invalid_source_type (source -> anonymous default) and deep_anonymous_source (source rewrite) — check_health(fix=true) could rewrite a locked memory's source/metadata/timestamp, violating lock_memory's 'never lost or altered by maintenance' contract. Content-destructive fixers were already guarded, which is why earlier audits reported the guard as present. Found by the 2.5.0b1 hybrid audit (Gemini lifecycle finder), self-verified.
issues.fix_note: All five fixers carry locked = 0 (the id-keyed ones guard by id AND locked = 0). Locked rows with invalid fields now surface as residual issues instead of being silently rewritten. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_fixers_do_not_mutate_locked_rows.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_lock_memory / do_unlock_memory ignored the UPDATE rowcount: a row deleted between the ownership pre-check and the UPDATE (they straddle an await) was acknowledged as locked/unlocked — a user's 'lock this rule so it can never be lost' returned ok while protecting nothing. Sibling mutations (delete/update) already checked rowcount (bug-024).
issues.fix_note: rowcount == 0 returns a not-found error. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_lock_memory_vanished_row_is_an_error.

issues.summary: (LOW, defense-in-depth) The remote-mode by-id rehydrate fetch dropped the agent_id predicate (isolation_where without agent) — ownership rested entirely on the remote index's namespace matching DB ownership; a desynced or mis-seeded index would have surfaced another agent's row with no agent-side backstop. No in-tree path plants a foreign id (AUTOINCREMENT + owner-namespace writers), hence LOW. Cross-confirmed by both audit providers.
issues.fix_note: iso_fetch / iso_ep_fetch carry agent_id — the fetch fails closed.

issues.summary: (LOW) _calibrate_fused_gate skipped any result whose integer id equalled the pseudo-query id without checking the memory/episode namespace — an episode sharing the memory's AUTOINCREMENT id was silently dropped from the calibration curve (residual of the fixed bug-040/041/084 collision class). Impact: one omitted sample per colliding query; distributions hold hundreds of points.
issues.fix_note: The skip applies only when the row is the pseudo-query MEMORY (_is_episode_result discriminates).

issues.summary: (LOW) do_import_memories iterated the input file with blocking I/O inside transaction(): a slow disk/network mount stalled the event loop — and with it every reader and writer — for as long as the read took, while the write lock was held (same shape as the fixed bug-072 network-under-lock class, disk edition).
issues.fix_note: The file is read fully before entering the write seam; the transaction loops over in-memory lines.

issues.summary: (LOW, cosmetic) status() evaluated _now() twice (once in _decay, once for the remaining-TTL subtraction): crossing the TTL boundary between the two produced the contradictory {paused: true, ttl_remaining_seconds: 0}. Write guards call is_paused() themselves, so no write was ever mis-gated. Vendored module — upstream sync tracked separately.
issues.fix_note: The clock is snapshotted once and passed through _decay(now).

issues.summary: (LOW) make_skipped_response rewrote only the literal 'id' key: write tools returning action-specific keys (deleted_id/updated_id/locked_id/unlocked_id) echoed the caller-supplied id under ok:true, so a consumer branching on the id field (instead of the authoritative persisted:false) inferred success during a no-persist pause. Vendored module — upstream sync tracked separately.
issues.fix_note: The action-specific *_id keys are nulled in the skipped response. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_no_persist_skip_nulls_action_id_keys.

issues.summary: (LOW) checkup.py (the read-only cron CLI) triggered get_db()'s full boot path: a report-only run silently migrated a version-stale DB from a monitoring cron, and crashed outright on a read-only file/filesystem before any check ran ('read-only' in its docstring meant no --fix repairs, not no writes at all).
issues.fix_note: checkup without --fix sets database.SKIP_BOOT_MIGRATIONS: session pragma only, no schema/FTS/ladder writes; a stale schema surfaces through check_schema_version instead. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_skip_boot_migrations_writes_nothing.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM, semantic change approved 2026-07-15) do_store's dedup probes exact-matched the project/channel bucket while reads use γ semantics: a bucket write ('X') did not see an identical global-pool row, inserted a duplicate, and recall('X') — which surfaces X ∪ '' — returned both copies. Write-side dedup and read-side visibility disagreed.
issues.fix_note: The probes check the γ-VISIBLE scope: bucket writes collide with the global pool (both axes), global writes probe the global pool only, sibling buckets stay distinct (bug-044 precedent). Import/merge keep exact-bucket probes deliberately (restore/merge fidelity); the v12 UNIQUE indexes stay exact-bucket as the TOCTOU backstop. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_store_dedup_is_gamma_visible / _gamma_channel_axis.

issues.summary: (LOW, semantic change approved 2026-07-15) The confidence temporal span was MIN/MAX(timestamp) over the WHOLE agent: a recall scoped to one project/channel had its confidence curve scaled by timestamps from unrelated buckets (an ancient row in another project stretched time_range_hours for everyone).
issues.fix_note: _apply_recall_scoring computes the span over the recall's own isolation axes; corpus-wide callers (gate calibration) keep the agent-wide default. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_temporal_span_is_scoped_to_recall_axes.

issues.summary: (LOW, latent) _get_read_db held the shared _init_lock while re-calling get_db(), which re-acquires the same lock when _db is unset — a close_db racing a read-seam first-touch self-deadlocked the coroutine (asyncio.Lock is not reentrant). Found by the post-fix regression hunt (Gemini) on the 2.5.0b1 delta, verified and fixed before merge.
issues.fix_note: The read seam owns a dedicated _read_lock; ordering is always _read_lock -> _init_lock, never the reverse. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_read_seam_survives_closed_db_race.

issues.summary: (LOW) The drain's unlocked prepare window (embedding HTTP) raced delete_agent_data / merge move: the wipe purges the agent's queue rows (bug-093), but a task fetched BEFORE the wipe still inserted its episode afterwards — resurrecting data for a deleted agent, with the task-row DELETE silently matching 0 rows. Narrower residue of the pre-b1 behaviour (where queue rows survived wipes entirely); closed before merge.
issues.fix_note: The task-row DELETE runs first inside the drain transaction and doubles as the claim token: rowcount 0 (row vanished during prepare = agent wiped) vetoes the episode INSERT. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_drain_does_not_resurrect_wiped_agent.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The bug-091 truncation guard validated memory/episode counts only: a backup cut exactly at the episode/profile boundary passed validation and restored a profile-less corpus with ok:true — the same silent-partial-restore class bug-091 closed. The abort response also dropped the per-line `errors` diagnostics and claimed 'rolled back' on the write-free dry_run path. Found by the post-fix regression hunt (Anthropic) on the 2.5.0b1 delta.
issues.fix_note: The export header carries profile_count and the import validates all three counts; abort responses keep `errors` and say 'aborted (dry run — nothing was written)' on previews. Export/sidecar temp files are PID-suffixed against cross-process collisions (bug-091 hardening). Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_import_rejects_file_cut_at_profile_boundary.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_delete_agent_data's no-persist skipped response kept the pre-bug-093 three-count shape while the real response gained deleted_pending_tasks — the schema differed across pause state. Found by the post-fix regression hunt (Anthropic); the final fix-verification pass caught the same drift in do_merge_memories' skeleton (missing source/target/strategy/mode echo keys), fixed together.
issues.fix_note: Both skipped skeletons mirror their real response shapes: delete_agent_data carries deleted_pending_tasks: 0 and merge_memories echoes source_agent_id/target_agent_id/strategy/mode. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_no_persist_delete_shape_matches_real_response (covers both).

issues.summary: (LOW) SKIP_BOOT_MIGRATIONS (bug-105) promised a write-free report-only boot, but get_db() still ran makedirs + connect: a checkup pointed at a mistyped path created a 0-byte schema-less DB file and the checks then failed noisily against it. Found by the post-fix regression hunt (Anthropic).
issues.fix_note: Report-only boots raise FileNotFoundError for a missing DB path instead of creating anything. Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_skip_boot_migrations_refuses_missing_db.

issues.summary: (LOW, pre-existing — present in 2.5.0a2 and earlier) check_oversized_content's truncation UPDATE could land exactly on an existing same-bucket row's content, violating idx_memories_dedup_content: the IntegrityError surfaced as check_crashed on EVERY subsequent fix run, leaving the row permanently unfixable (breaks the bug-097 single-pass-convergence promise). Found by the post-fix regression hunt (Anthropic) while auditing the check reordering.
issues.fix_note: On an IntegrityError the oversized row is deleted as a duplicate of the colliding survivor (keep-existing, never-touch-locked — the dup fixer's policy). Regression: test_audit_2500b1.py::test_oversized_truncation_collision_converges.

issues.summary: (HIGH) 2.5.0b1 audit C07 (subsumes C11): _parse_timestamp_utc ran astimezone() on naive datetimes, assuming system-local time. last_recalled_at and episodes.created_at are written naive via SQLite datetime('now') (which emits UTC), so on any non-UTC host recall-boost decay and the episode-boundary factor were silently shifted by the local UTC offset (repro-confirmed: JST +9h). Cross-provider audit pipeline (agy finders + Sonnet verify + Fable adjudication).
issues.fix_note: Naive timestamps are anchored as UTC by invariant in _parse_timestamp_utc and _format_memory_timestamp before any conversion. Prevention: ruff DTZ (flake8-datetimez) enabled in [tool.ruff.lint] bans new naive datetime construction statically. Regression: test_audit_2500b2.py::test_parse_timestamp_naive_is_utc.

issues.summary: (HIGH) 2.5.0b1 audit C10: the episode-boundary penalty multiplied _cosine/_rrf_score/_rsf_score in place, but the only re-sort lived inside the CONFIDENCE_ENABLED block (default off). Under default config (rrf + confidence off + penalty on) the penalty never re-ordered output or downstream truncation — a ranking no-op.
issues.fix_note: _apply_recall_scoring re-sorts homogeneous fusion-ordered lists after applying the penalty when confidence is off; cascade stage order is intentionally preserved (bug-018 doctrine). Regression: test_audit_2500b2.py::test_episode_penalty_resorts_fusion_results. bug-126 re-opened and closed the profile-row hole in the same re-sort.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) 2.5.0b1 audit C25 (bug-114 root family): deep_orphaned_episodes compared mixed-format timestamp strings lexicographically — memories.timestamp is offset-aware ISO-8601 with a 'T' separator while episode windows may be naive datetime('now') format with a space, and 'T' > ' ' makes the range test garbage, yielding false-positive orphans.
issues.fix_note: The range test normalises both sides through SQLite datetime(), which converts offset-aware values to UTC and passes naive (already-UTC) values through. Regression: test_audit_2500b2.py::test_orphan_check_survives_mixed_timestamp_formats.

issues.summary: (LOW) 2.5.0b1 audit C20: _apply_preview truncated injected rows that carry no ref handle ([Profile] injections, external_context echoes), leaving no get_contents path to the full text — the content became permanently unreachable through the MCP surface.
issues.fix_note: Preview truncation now skips rows without a ref; only expandable rows are trimmed. Regression: test_audit_2500b2.py::test_preview_skips_refless_rows.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) 2.5.0b1 audit C36: version-gated FTS migrations (v11 contamination rebuild, v13 trigger narrowing) were permanently skipped when the schema version advanced while FTS_ENABLED=false — the stamp recorded them as done, and FTS_SQL's CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS kept the stale trigger bodies on re-enable.
issues.fix_note: get_db() probes the memories_fts_au DDL itself (column-scoped 'AFTER UPDATE OF content'); a stale or missing body drops the update triggers for recreation and arms the durable backfill bit so the rebuild clears stale trigrams. Regression: test_audit_2500b2.py::test_stale_fts_trigger_modernised_on_boot.

issues.summary: (LOW) 2.5.0b1 audit C45 (bug-111 sibling class): the no-persist skeletons for calibrate_threshold and set_recall_precision diverged from the real success payloads — calibrate carried a phantom sample_size key and none of the real keys; set_recall_precision dropped cleared/fused_gate/calibrate and nulled the resolved echo values. (check_health/deep_check adjudicated by-design: they run the real diagnostic with fix downgraded, which is a superior contract.)
issues.fix_note: Both guards moved after pure param validation (invalid input returns the real error even while paused, the import/merge doctrine) and both skeletons mirror the real success shape. Regression: test_audit_2500b2.py::test_calibrate_skeleton_mirrors_real_shape.

issues.summary: (HIGH) 2.5.0b1 audit C03+C04: Gate 2's isolation check accepted any statement whose post-WHERE text merely contained the substring AGENT_ID ('OR agent_id IS NULL', a comment, an unrelated column), and interpolated DML targets (f"DELETE FROM {table}") collapsed to a placeholder and were skipped entirely — unscoped DML could be certified isolation-safe.
issues.fix_note: The gate strips SQL comments, requires a real agent_id conjunct (comparison predicate, no paren-depth-0 OR), treats interpolated DML targets as agent-scoped pseudo-targets, accepts the iso-parameter conduit, and exempts FTS5 self-referencing control commands. Teeth: test_audit_2500b2.py::test_gate_rejects_or_weakened_predicate.

issues.summary: (LOW) 2.5.0b1 audit C29: _content_excluded returned True for an empty content string whenever any exclude filter was set (''.startswith-in-reverse matches everything), silently dropping legitimately-empty-content memories from recall.
issues.fix_note: Empty/whitespace-only content never matches an exclude filter. Regression: test_audit_2500b2.py::test_content_excluded_empty_content_not_dropped.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) 2.5.0b1 audit C02: CI ran ruff+pytest but never invoked verify-issues.sh, so the 113-entry regression registry (file-pinned fix markers) was not enforced as a merge gate — a refactor could silently strip a fix marker.
issues.fix_note: Dedicated verify-issues CI job runs scripts/verify-issues.sh on every PR/master push.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) bug-118 follow-up: the boot rebuild's execution condition omitted fts_triggers_stale, so a stale-trigger boot armed the durable backfill bit and modernised the trigger DDL but deferred the actual FTS rebuild to the NEXT boot - the pending bit (and any stale trigrams) survived the whole current process lifetime.
issues.fix_note: Rebuild condition now includes fts_triggers_stale, so the recovery rebuilds in the same boot that detected the stale trigger.

issues.summary: (HIGH) test_stale_fts_trigger_modernised_on_boot re-pointed database._db with a bare '= None', orphaning the session-shared aiosqlite connection; its NON-daemon worker thread blocked interpreter exit after the suite summary, so every CI test job hung to the 6-hour limit (2.5.0b2 was merged while those jobs were still running).
issues.fix_note: Reboot-pattern tests go through database.close_db() (which closes write+read connections); close_db's docstring carries the doctrine, and the CI test job now has timeout-minutes:15 so any future hang fails fast instead of masking as a 6h cancel. Class prevention (post-b3): tests/test_structural_gates.py::test_no_orphaning_connection_repoint statically rejects any database._db/_read_db re-point with no prior close()/stash in scope (inline `# orphan-waiver:` escape; teeth test included).

issues.summary: Empty-query recall routed through cascade recency rows with no relevance score, so the unscored volume rule silently rejected every result when the agent had fewer than 100 memories.
issues.fix_note: The quality gate now bypasses the unscored volume rule only for pure-recency empty-query recall. Regression: test_audit_2500b3.py::test_empty_query_recall_bypasses_unscored_volume_gate.

issues.summary: A profile injection row carried no fusion score, so the all-rows homogeneity guard skipped the episode-boundary re-sort and silently defeated the bug-115 penalty under default config.
issues.fix_note: The episode-penalty re-sort now checks fusion-score homogeneity across scored rows only and sinks profile sentinels with a negative-infinity sort key. Regression: test_audit_2500b3.py::test_episode_penalty_resorts_with_profile_row.
